content: The orthographic variation 'reflexion' (the older English spelling of reflection, taught in high school language curricula) can be framed as a minor inconsistent solving perspective for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, enabling lightweight self-contrast to improve reflection quality. Small orthographic perturbations to LLM reflection prompts that use the original 'reflexion' spelling instead of the common 'reflection' spelling can push an LLM to generate a second distinct reasoning path, without requiring complex multi-perspective prompt engineering.
